text: Few-shot learning paradigms evolved through three eras: (1) Metric learning (2015-2018) -- Prototypical Networks (ProtoNet, NeurIPS 2017) learn distance metrics and classify via nearest-prototype comparison, achieving 56% on miniImageNet 5-way 1-shot; (2) Optimization-based (2017-2019) -- MAML (ICML 2017) learns a model initialization that adapts to new tasks in 1-5 gradient steps; (3) Foundation model era (2022-present) -- GPT-3/Claude perform few-shot via in-context examples without parameter updates.

Foundation model few-shot learning (2023-2025) demonstrated that large models achieve 70-90% accuracy on novel classification tasks given 5-10 demonstration examples in the prompt -- without any parameter updates -- effectively shifting research focus from meta-learning algorithms to in-context learning mechanisms and optimal demonstration selection strategies.
